evidence_summary: Complete overlap of established 8p23.1 recurrent microdeletion region (ClinGen Dosage Sensitivity HI = sufficient evidence). Detected concordantly by SNP array, optical genome mapping, and PacBio HiFi long-read sequencing in a 4-year-old girl with microcephaly, growth restriction, global developmental delay, and seizures.
clinical_features: Microcephaly; growth restriction; global developmental delay (Bayley-III Cognitive 65 / Language 56 / Motor 61); seizures with posterior shift on serial EEG; small head circumference at birth; normal myelination on brain MRI; normal echocardiogram

evidence_summary: 4.3-Mb deletion at 8p22 encompassing ~40 protein-coding genes including TUSC3. Detected concordantly by SNP array, optical genome mapping, and PacBio HiFi long-read sequencing. Partial overlap with literature on 8p22 deletions in NDD.
clinical_features: Microcephaly; growth restriction; global developmental delay; seizures
acmg_amp_criteria_applied: ACMG/ClinGen 2020 (Riggs et al.): 1A; 3B (25-49 genes, +0.45); 4O (literature overlap, +0.10) -> +0.55 Likely pathogenic

evidence_summary: Apparent inv(7)(q11.21q22) on G-banded karyotype refined by integrated optical genome mapping and PacBio HiFi long-read sequencing into a paracentric inversion with the proximal breakpoint at chr7:69,938,691 (intron 2 of AUTS2 [NM_015570.4]) and the distal partner at chr7:95,720,897 (7q21.3). Predicted to result in loss-of-function of AUTS2 (ClinGen Dosage Sensitivity HI score 3, sufficient evidence for autosomal-dominant haploinsufficiency). Patient is a 30-year-old man with intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, micrognathia, and scoliosis — features consistent with AUTS2-related syndrome (OMIM 615834). Prior whole-exome sequencing was non-diagnostic; retrospective short-read SV re-analysis confirmed both breakpoints fall outside exome capture territory.
clinical_features: Intellectual disability; autism spectrum disorder; ADHD; motor and language delay (childhood); schizophrenia (adulthood); micrognathia; unilateral hearing impairment; scoliosis
acmg_amp_criteria_applied: ACMG/AMP 2015: PVS1 (predicted LoF in established HI gene with AD inheritance) + PM6 (de novo by parental karyotype, not molecularly confirmed) + PP4 (highly specific phenotype) -> Pathogenic

evidence_summary: 5.4-Mb deletion at 7q34-q35 encompassing 15 OMIM-listed genes (BRAF, AGK, CLCN1, TBXAS1, WEE2, SSBP1, TAS2R38, PRSS1, PRSS2, TRPV6, KEL, CASP2, NOBOX, TPK1, SLC37A3) and ~45 protein-coding genes total. Identified by SNP array, optical genome mapping, and PacBio HiFi long-read sequencing in a 3-year-old boy with global developmental delay, autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, cryptorchidism, and micropenis. Co-occurs with a CNTNAP2-disrupting inversion in the same proband as part of a complex multi-chromosomal genomic rearrangement (chromosomes 2, 5, 7, 13) with features suggestive of chromoanagenesis.
clinical_features: Global developmental delay (Bayley-III Cognitive 75 / Language 65 / Motor 79); autism spectrum disorder; ADHD; cryptorchidism; micropenis
acmg_amp_criteria_applied: ACMG/ClinGen 2020 (Riggs et al.): 1A; 3B (25-49 genes, +0.45); 4O (literature overlap with 7q33-q36 NDD cases, +0.15) -> +0.60 Likely pathogenic

evidence_summary: 1.55-Mb inversion at 7q35 with the distal breakpoint at chr7:146,600,257 truncating CNTNAP2 (ClinGen Dosage Sensitivity HI score 2; AD risk for autism spectrum disorder and language impairment). Identified by integrated optical genome mapping and PacBio HiFi long-read sequencing in the context of a complex multi-chromosomal rearrangement. Co-occurs with a 7q34-q35 deletion in the same proband.
clinical_features: Global developmental delay; autism spectrum disorder; ADHD; cryptorchidism; micropenis
acmg_amp_criteria_applied: ACMG/AMP 2015: PVS1_moderate (predicted LoF in CNTNAP2 with HI evidence) + PM6 (de novo by parental karyotype, not molecularly confirmed) + PP4 (ASD/language phenotype matches) -> Likely pathogenic
